key_insight: GeoSD improves reasoning generalization by making self-distillation trust the student's current support and by limiting how far predictions drift over training in the same distribution geometry.
engineering_relevance: For post-training large language models, the method offers a practical way to keep in-distribution gains from self-distillation while reducing overconfident out-of-distribution failures.
method: Hellinger-weighted distillation down-weights teacher preferences that the student cannot yet justify.
A proximal regularizer constrains prediction drift from a recent checkpoint using Fisher-Rao distance.
Natural-gradient updates are used so optimization follows the geometry of next-token distributions.
evidence_signal: Across mathematical reasoning benchmarks and three model families, GeoSD improves average OOD accuracy by 5.7-8.6 points over the base model, with results reported from 1.7B to 32B models.
watch_item: The approach adds geometric regularization and checkpoint-based constraints, so implementation needs careful tuning to avoid reducing the gains of standard self-distillation.
